hypothesis: If the Force Delta (U∆) between conservative and non-conservative force predictions for a neural network interatomic potential is large, then the RMS error between predicted non-conservative forces and ground-truth DFT forces (εdirect) will be large, across both equilibrium and adversarially perturbed out-of-distribution structures, demonstrating that U∆ provides a fast, data-free uncertainty estimate of the model’s force prediction accuracy.
